items.label: Invalid / unknown
items.description: Reserved sentinel: tooling replaces LLM outputs that are not valid positioning_tag ids so ingest can continue.

items.label: Interdisciplinary
items.description: Positioning emphasizes cross-disciplinary work.

items.label: Mech Eng Adjacent
items.description: Emphasizes work adjacent to mechanical engineering.

items.label: HCI Adjacent
items.description: Emphasizes work adjacent to human computer interaction.

items.label: UI/UX
items.description: Emphasizes user interface and user experience design.

items.label: Fine Arts Adjacent
items.description: Emphasizes work adjacent to the fine arts.

items.label: Humanities Adjacent
items.description: Emphasizes work adjacent to the humanities.

items.label: Architecture Adjacent
items.description: Emphasizes work adjacent to architecture.

items.label: Public Policy Adjacent
items.description: Emphasizes work adjacent to public policy.

items.label: Media Adjacent
items.description: Emphasizes work adjacent to new media technologies.

items.label: Entrepreneurship Adjacent
items.description: Emphasizes work adjacent to entrepreneurship.

items.label: Professional
items.description: Emphasizes industry readiness, studios, and professional skills.

items.label: Research
items.description: Emphasizes academic research trajectories and scholarly output.

items.label: Social Impact
items.description: Emphasizes public interest, equity, sustainability, or civic contexts.

items.label: Physical Making
items.description: Emphasizes physical making, fabrication, or production.

items.label: Digital Making
items.description: Emphasizes digital products, services, platforms, or apps.

items.label: Emerging Technologies
items.description: explicitly emphasizes emerging technologies.

items.label: Critical / Speculative Design
items.description: explicitly emphasizes critical or speculative design.

items.label: Interaction Design
items.description: explicitly emphasizes interaction design.

items.label: Industrial / Product Design
items.description: Emphasizes industrial or product design.

items.label: Service design
items.description: Emphasizes service systems, journeys, touchpoints, and delivery across organizational or public contexts.

items.label: Strategic design
items.description: Emphasizes design for strategy, foresight, organizational change, or long-horizon planning.

items.label: Sustainability
items.description: Emphasizes environmental, social, and economic sustainability in design goals, materials, or outcomes.

items.label: Systems thinking
items.description: Emphasizes holistic, systemic, or complexity-aware framing of problems and interventions.

items.label: Human-centered design
items.description: Emphasizes people-first, participatory, or inclusive design process and outcomes (distinct from HCI as a neighboring discipline).

items.label: Invalid / unknown
items.description: Reserved sentinel: tooling replaces LLM outputs that are not valid course_type ids so ingest can continue.

items.label: Design studio
items.description: A project-based studio course that normally carries larger curricular weight than other courses.
rules: Use only when the course is clearly a project-based studio.
Do not classify a course as design_studio merely because it includes a project.

Use design_studio only when normalized_unit_weight is strictly greater than 1.1; at 1.1 or below (or when weight is unknown), use design_methods_systems instead. Higher weight above 1.1 should increase the likelihood of choosing design_studio when the course is clearly a heavy studio/project.
Design studio can never be hybrid.
If primary_type is design_studio, secondary_type must be null.

items.label: Human factors
items.description: Courses focused on people, communities, users, behavior, culture, social context, design research, ethnography, ergonomics, or human-centered design.

items.label: Technology
items.description: Courses focused on technical design methods, computational tools, software, hardware, fabrication, sensors, AI, robotics, CAD, optimization, or technologies used in the design itself.

items.label: History, theory, and positioning
items.description: Courses focused on design history, design theory, critical theory, contemporary issues, ethics, politics, movements, disciplinary framing, or colloquia.

items.label: Rhetoric, storytelling, and presentation
items.description: Courses focused on writing, presenting, communicating, documenting, exhibiting, pitching, or narrating design work.

items.label: Design methods and systems
items.description: Courses focused on design process, structured methods, systems thinking, strategy, problem framing, research synthesis, service design methods, participatory design, systems mapping, or design frameworks.

items.label: Entrepreneurship
items.description: Courses focused on venture creation, innovation management, commercialization, markets, business models, product-market fit, leadership, startup practice, or go-to-market strategy.

items.label: Open or other
items.description: Use when a course or elective slot is broad, unspecified, intentionally flexible, or does not clearly map to one of the defined types.

items.label: Invalid / unknown
items.description: Reserved sentinel for values that are not valid host_academic_model ids.

items.label: co-hosted by design + engineering + business units
items.description: Program is administratively housed across design, engineering, and business units.

items.label: co-hosted by engineering + design units
items.description: Program is administratively housed in both engineering and design units.

items.label: co-hosted by engineering + fine arts units
items.description: Program is administratively housed in both engineering and arts units.

items.label: co-hosted by engineering + business units
items.description: Program is administratively housed in both engineering and business units.

items.label: co-hosted by design + business units
items.description: Program is administratively housed in both design and business units.

items.label: hosted in an engineering unit
items.description: Administered by an engineering school or college.

items.label: hosted in a design unit
items.description: Administered by a design school or college.

items.label: hosted in an arts / humanities units
items.description: Administered by a unit in arts, humanities, or fine arts.

items.label: hosted in an business / management unit
items.description: Administered by a unit in business or management.

items.label: hosted in an CS / information technology unit
items.description: Administered by a unit in computer science or information technology.

items.label: Unknown
items.description: Academic unit is not clearly associated with any known categories.

items.label: Invalid / unknown
items.description: Reserved sentinel for values that are not valid duration_category ids.

items.label: Less than 2 semesters
items.description: Program length is less than two standard academic semesters.

items.label: Two semesters
items.description: Roughly two standard academic semesters.

items.label: Three semesters
items.description: Roughly three standard academic semesters.

items.label: Four semesters
items.description: Roughly four standard academic semesters.

items.label: Four or more semesters
items.description: Extended programs spanning more than four standard academic semesters.

items.label: Variable or custom
items.description: Duration is student-paced, modular, or not expressed in standard terms.

items.label: Invalid / unknown
items.description: Reserved sentinel for values that are not valid sequencedness ids.

items.label: Highly sequenced
items.description: A large majority of required curriculum items (especially core courses) have an official semester or term placement in degree-plan materials—not only a broad year label.
rules: Operational rule: among required courses (or plan rows) you can list from evidence, compute the share that name a **specific semester or term** (e.g. Fall Year 1, Spring Year 2). If **roughly one-half (50%) or more** have that level of placement, prefer **highly_sequenced**.

items.label: Partially sequenced
items.description: Meaningful sequencing (e.g. a prescribed first-year core or prerequisites) but a substantial portion of required or listed courses lack an explicit semester/term in the materials you have, or placement stops at year-level.
rules: If **fewer than ~two-thirds** of enumerated required items show a **specific term** (many are only "Year 1 core", placeholders, or undated catalog lines), prefer **partially_sequenced** over highly_sequenced.
Also use partially_sequenced when only **some** threads are term-mapped while others are intentionally open across terms.

items.label: Flexible
items.description: Minimal prescribed term-by-term sequencing for required work; students choose timing across terms within broad requirements.
rules: Use when evidence shows few or no required courses pinned to particular semesters, beyond generic credit progress rules.

items.label: Invalid / unknown
items.description: Reserved sentinel for values that are not valid unit_system ids.

items.label: Semester credit hours
items.description: US-style semester credits.

items.label: Quarter units
items.description: US-style quarter-system units.

items.label: ECTS
items.description: European Credit Transfer and Accumulation System.

items.label: Other or mixed
items.description: Nonstandard or explicitly mixed unit systems.

items.label: INVALID
items.description: Sentinel: the provided CIP code was not recognized. Repair to a valid id.

items.label: unknown
items.description: CIP code could not be determined from available evidence.

items.label: Architecture
items.description: Programs that prepare individuals to design buildings, structures, and built environments. Graduate programs in architecture typically lead to M.Arch degrees.

items.label: City/Urban, Community, and Regional Planning
items.description: Programs focused on the planning and design of urban environments, communities, and regional spaces.

items.label: Environmental Design/Architecture
items.description: Programs that integrate ecological and environmental concerns into design and spatial planning.

items.label: Sustainable Design/Architecture
items.description: Programs focused on sustainable approaches to the design of buildings, products, and systems.

items.label: Interior Architecture
items.description: Programs that focus on the design and planning of interior spaces as architectural environments.

items.label: Digital Communication and Media/Multimedia
items.description: Programs covering the production and distribution of digital content across platforms and media channels.

items.label: Communication Management and Strategic Communications
items.description: Programs focused on planning and managing organizational communication strategies.

items.label: Computer and Information Sciences, General
items.description: General programs covering the theory and practice of computing, information systems, and digital technologies.

items.label: Informatics
items.description: Programs integrating computing, information science, and domain expertise; often human- or organization-centered.

items.label: Human-Centered Technology Design
items.description: Programs explicitly focused on designing technology for human needs, overlapping with HCI and UX.

items.label: Information Science/Studies
items.description: Programs covering the organization, retrieval, and management of information; includes library and information science traditions.

items.label: Computer Science
items.description: Programs focused on the theory, design, and implementation of software and computational systems.

items.label: Web Page, Digital/Multimedia and Information Resources Design
items.description: Programs focused on the design and production of digital and multimedia content for web and interactive environments.

items.label: Modeling, Virtual Environments and Simulation
items.description: Programs covering 3D modeling, simulation, VR/AR, and virtual environment design.

items.label: Engineering, General
items.description: General engineering programs not confined to a single discipline; used for interdisciplinary or integrated engineering degrees.

items.label: Mechanical Engineering
items.description: Programs covering the design, analysis, and manufacturing of mechanical systems and devices.

items.label: Systems Engineering
items.description: Programs focused on the design and management of complex integrated systems across disciplines.

items.label: Industrial Engineering
items.description: Programs focused on optimizing processes, systems, and organizations through engineering analysis and design.

items.label: Mechatronics, Robotics, and Automation Engineering
items.description: Programs integrating mechanical, electrical, and computing engineering for robotics and automated systems design.

items.label: Engineering, Other
items.description: Engineering programs not classifiable under a standard 14.xx code; use when an engineering program doesn't fit a named specialty.

items.label: 3-D Modeling and Design Technology/Technician
items.description: Programs focused on 3D design, digital fabrication, and spatial representation using CAD/CAM tools.

items.label: Engineering Design
items.description: Programs explicitly focused on the design process within engineering contexts; covers product and systems design.

items.label: Visual and Performing Arts, General
items.description: General programs spanning the visual and performing arts; used when a program doesn't fit a narrower arts category.

items.label: Digital Arts
items.description: Programs focused on art and design created with or for digital technologies and environments.

items.label: Design and Visual Communications, General
items.description: Primary CIP code for graduate design programs focused on communication, interaction, visual systems, or multidisciplinary design practice. Includes MDes programs not better classified under a specific design specialty.

items.label: Commercial and Advertising Art
items.description: Programs focused on art and design for commercial, marketing, and advertising purposes.

items.label: Industrial and Product Design
items.description: Programs focused on the design of physical products, manufacturing processes, and human-object interaction.

items.label: Fashion/Apparel Design
items.description: Programs focused on the design of clothing, accessories, and wearable products.

items.label: Interior Design
items.description: Programs focused on the design of interior spaces for function, aesthetics, and human experience.

items.label: Graphic Design
items.description: Programs focused on visual communication through typography, layout, and image composition for print and digital media.

items.label: Illustration
items.description: Programs focused on image-making for editorial, narrative, or commercial contexts.

items.label: Game and Interactive Media Design
items.description: Programs focused on the design of games, interactive experiences, and immersive media.

items.label: Design and Applied Arts, Other
items.description: Design programs not classifiable under a specific 50.04xx code; use for interdisciplinary or emerging design specializations.

items.label: Film/Cinema/Media Studies
items.description: Programs covering the study, production, and criticism of film and media.

items.label: Fine/Studio Arts, General
items.description: Programs focused on studio art practice across media; used for fine arts graduate programs without a specific medium specialization.

items.label: Intermedia/Multimedia
items.description: Programs exploring art and design across multiple media or at the intersection of disciplines.

items.label: Arts, Entertainment, and Media Management, General
items.description: Programs covering business and management of arts, media, and entertainment organizations.

items.label: Community/Environmental/Socially-Engaged Art
items.description: Programs centered on art and design practice engaged with community, social justice, or environmental concerns.

items.label: Visual and Performing Arts, Other
items.description: Visual and performing arts programs not classifiable under any specific 50.xx code.

items.label: Business Administration and Management, General
items.description: General graduate business programs (e.g., MBA); used when a program has a primary business administration framing.

items.label: Project Management
items.description: Programs focused on the planning, execution, and management of projects in organizational contexts.

items.label: Science/Technology Management
items.description: Programs at the intersection of scientific or technical expertise and organizational management.

items.label: Entrepreneurship/Entrepreneurial Studies
items.description: Programs focused on new venture creation, innovation, and entrepreneurial thinking.

items.label: Management Information Systems, General
items.description: Programs integrating business management with information systems and technology strategy.

items.label: Management Science
items.description: Programs applying quantitative and analytical methods to organizational decision-making and strategy.
